markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define receipts for extraction success, partial success, unsupported format, missing text layer, OCR needed, and operator repair. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: ExtractionReceiptModel leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ExtractionReceiptModel is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 ce857886; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: MEDIUM: receipts.receipt_event_id (0162) is NULLABLE (TEXT REFERENCES kernel_event_ledger ... ON DELETE RESTRICT, no NOT NULL). The engine always passes Some(&event_id) so live receipts are ledger-paired, but the DB does NOT enforce 'every receipt has a ledger receipt'. A future/alternate caller could insert a ledger-less receipt and pass the CHECKs. Spec law (every mutation leaves an EventLedger receipt) is APPLICATION-enforced here, not schema-enforced. Recommend NOT NULL or a trigger if the law must be unbreakable.
NewExtractionReceipt::validate() mirrors the 0162 CHECKs (success<=>no error_class; non-success<=>error_class; non-negative counts) so callers fail before touching the DB — good fail-fast.
ON DELETE RESTRICT on receipt_event_id correctly prevents deleting a ledger event out from under a receipt (audit integrity).
evasion_probes: non-success receipt without error_class -> rejected by validate() AND DB chk_*_error_shape CONFIRMED.
success receipt WITH error_class -> rejected by validate() AND DB chk_*_success_shape CONFIRMED.
negative span/redaction counts -> rejected (validate + DB CHECK >=0) CONFIRMED.
empty extractor_id/version -> rejected (validate + DB btrim CHECK) CONFIRMED.
every status (success/partial/failed/deferred/skipped/blocked) round-trips through FromStr and the DB CHECK vocabulary CONFIRMED.
SecretBlocked + UnsupportedFormat are is_repairable()==false (correctly NOT queued) CONFIRMED — a blocked secret never enters the repair retry loop.

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG negative test green (8 hardening tests + 70 unit, no regression).

evidence: Hardened 5876feaf: ingestion secret-leak/lopdf/dead-letter fixes verified; boundary-seam fixture shows ZERO raw secret bytes in spans; DB redaction guard (0210) + receipt NOT NULL (0211) enforced.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-085 requires a typed ExtractionReceiptModel for every extraction attempt (success/partial/unsupported/no-text-layer/OCR/operator-repair) with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, anchored to Master Spec 2.3.13.11 KnowledgeSource last-index receipt. The committed code implements a real, durable, PostgreSQL-backed receipt model with typed status + 10 typed error classes, app-side and DB-side shape CHECKs, EventLedger receipt binding, and per-source rollup advancement, proven by real-managed-PG integration tests (no SQLite, no mocks).
findings: receipts.rs:19-129 defines ExtractionStatus (success/partial/failed/deferred/skipped/blocked) and IngestionErrorClass with the exact MT-085 vocabulary (UNSUPPORTED_FORMAT, NO_TEXT_LAYER, OCR_NEEDED, ...) plus repairability classification (is_repairable excludes UnsupportedFormat/SecretBlocked) feeding the MT-094 repair queue.
receipts.rs:171-199 NewExtractionReceipt::validate() pre-validates shape app-side: non-empty extractor id/version, success<=>no error_class, non-success<=>error_class, non-negative counts.

migration 0162 (shown from commit ce857886) creates knowledge_ingestion_receipts in real PostgreSQL with matching CHECK constraints (chk_*_error_shape, chk_*_success_shape, count >=0, id/hash regex, btrim extractor) and a receipt_event_id FK to kernel_event_ledger(event_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT; registers the table as authority in knowledge_schema_registry with mt_id MT-085.

store.rs:263-333 record_extraction_receipt / list_extraction_receipts / get_extraction_receipt are real sqlx queries binding all 15 columns against the live pool (self.pool) -- genuine PostgreSQL persistence, not mock.

Real-PG proof: tests/knowledge_ingestion_pipeline_tests.rs:111-198 (mt085_mt090...) persists a receipt, asserts typed Success/no error_class/extractor identity, then opens env.pg.raw_connection() and queries kernel_event_ledger by receipt_event_id to confirm the receipt is bound to a replayable ledger event carrying actor/session/correlation, and asserts the per-source rollup (parser_status, extraction_status, last_index_receipt_event_id) advanced per spec 2.3.13.11. Harness (knowledge_ingestion_support.rs:4) is explicitly real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL, full migration chain, no SQLite, no mocks.
Sub-rule 1: no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable placeholders reachable on the receipt path (grep clean on receipts.rs + store.rs).
Sub-rule 2: durable receipt + EventLedger authority is exercised against the real Handshake-native PostgreSQL implementation, not implementer-authored mocks.
Sub-rule 3: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260611-071323, completed_by=null, status READY_FOR_VALIDATION -- no self-certified COMPLETED.

Prior adversarial concern (nullable receipt_event_id allowing a ledger-less receipt to bypass the receipt law) was actually hardened: migration 0211 backfills any legacy NULL via a synthetic ledger event then SET NOT NULL on receipt_event_id (and repair-queue enqueue_event_id), with a matching .down.sql relaxing it -- so 'every receipt has a ledger receipt' is now schema-enforced, matching spec 2.3.13.11's mutation-receipt law.
Aside (non-blocking): MT contract cites v02.192 spec path; resolved against current v02.194 where section 2.3.13.11 KnowledgeSource/last-index-receipt content is present and consistent.
evidence_checked: wtc-kernel-009/src/backend/handshake_core/tests/knowledge_ingestion_pipeline_tests.rs:111-198 (+ grep of receipt usage across pipeline tests)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
